meaning: n.<美>木材,无用的杂物(如破旧家俱),废物,隆隆声vt.砍伐木材,乱堆vi.伐木,喧闹地向前走,笨重地行动
examples: Let's bring in a truck-load of lumber and start building tomorrow.
The lumber and paper mills in Port Angeles also block access to upstream spawning beds.
Parliament used British tax money to pay modest incentives to Americans producing such items as silk, iron, dyes, hemp, and lumber.
Two lumber out and splash into the river, unconcerned by the presence of humans in a canoe.
In the early 1980s, Gilman Paper expanded into lumber production, founding Gilman Building Products Co.
